query: To help with moderation, please flag any comment that was posted by the same user on the same post less than 10 seconds after their previous comment. This is to identify potential rapid-fire spam.
clean_up_sqls: UPDATE tbl_comments SET is_flagged = FALSE WHERE is_flagged = TRUE;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    import datetime

    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list:
            return
        if isinstance(sql_list, str):
            sql_list = [sql_list]
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    def fetch_scalar(sql, params=None):
        cursor.execute(sql, params)
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        return None if row is None else row[0]

    # Test IDs
    test_user_id_rapid = 999991
    test_post_id_rapid = 999991
    test_user_id_slow = 999992
    test_post_id_slow = 999992

    comment_id_rapid1, comment_id_rapid2, comment_id_slow2 = None, None, None

    try:
        # === Setup: Insert test data ===
        now = datetime.datetime.utcnow()
        time1_rapid = now - datetime.timedelta(seconds=20)
        time2_rapid = time1_rapid + datetime.timedelta(seconds=5)
        time1_slow = now - datetime.timedelta(seconds=40)
        time2_slow = time1_slow + datetime.timedelta(seconds=15)

        # Insert test users (if foreign key exists)
        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_users (user_id, username) VALUES (%s, %s) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;",
            (test_user_id_rapid, 'rapid_user')
        )
        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_users (user_id, username) VALUES (%s, %s) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;",
            (test_user_id_slow, 'slow_user')
        )

        # Insert test posts (to satisfy foreign key)
        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, user_id, title, created_at) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;",
            (test_post_id_rapid, test_user_id_rapid, 'Rapid Post', now)
        )
        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, user_id, title, created_at) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;",
            (test_post_id_slow, test_user_id_slow, 'Slow Post', now)
        )

        # Insert comments
        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_comments (user_id, post_id, content, created_at, is_flagged) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s) RETURNING comment_id;",
            (test_user_id_rapid, test_post_id_rapid, 'First rapid comment', time1_rapid, False)
        )
        comment_id_rapid1 = cursor.fetchone()[0]

        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_comments (user_id, post_id, content, created_at, is_flagged) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s) RETURNING comment_id;",
            (test_user_id_rapid, test_post_id_rapid, 'Second rapid comment', time2_rapid, False)
        )
        comment_id_rapid2 = cursor.fetchone()[0]

        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_comments (user_id, post_id, content, created_at, is_flagged) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s);",
            (test_user_id_slow, test_post_id_slow, 'First slow comment', time1_slow, False)
        )
        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_comments (user_id, post_id, content, created_at, is_flagged) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s) RETURNING comment_id;",
            (test_user_id_slow, test_post_id_slow, 'Second slow comment', time2_slow, False)
        )
        comment_id_slow2 = cursor.fetchone()[0]

        conn.commit()

        # === Execute candidate SQL ===
        exec_many(pred_sqls)
        conn.commit()

        # === Assertions ===
        is_flagged_rapid = fetch_scalar("SELECT is_flagged FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id = %s;", (comment_id_rapid2,))
        assert is_flagged_rapid is True, f"FAIL: Rapid-fire comment (ID: {comment_id_rapid2}) was not flagged."

        is_flagged_rapid_first = fetch_scalar("SELECT is_flagged FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id = %s;", (comment_id_rapid1,))
        assert is_flagged_rapid_first is False, f"FAIL: First comment of rapid pair (ID: {comment_id_rapid1}) was incorrectly flagged."

        is_flagged_slow = fetch_scalar("SELECT is_flagged FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id = %s;", (comment_id_slow2,))
        assert is_flagged_slow is False, f"FAIL: Slow comment (ID: {comment_id_slow2}) was incorrectly flagged."

    finally:
        # === Cleanup ===
        cursor.execute("DELETE FROM tbl_comments WHERE user_id IN (%s, %s);", (test_user_id_rapid, test_user_id_slow))
        cursor.execute("DELETE FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id IN (%s, %s);", (test_post_id_rapid, test_post_id_slow))
        cursor.execute("DELETE FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id IN (%s, %s);", (test_user_id_rapid, test_user_id_slow))
        cursor.execute("UPDATE tbl_comments SET is_flagged = FALSE WHERE is_flagged = TRUE;")
        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()
sol_sql:
WITH rapid_comments AS (
    SELECT
        T1.comment_id,
        T1.created_at,
        -- Use LAG to get the timestamp of the previous comment by the same user on the same post
        LAG(T1.created_at, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY T1.user_id, T1.post_id ORDER BY T1.created_at ASC) AS previous_comment_time
    FROM
        tbl_comments AS T1
),
comments_to_flag AS (
    SELECT
        T2.comment_id
    FROM
        rapid_comments AS T2
    WHERE
        T2.previous_comment_time IS NOT NULL
        -- Check if the current comment was posted less than 10 seconds after the previous one
        AND T2.created_at - T2.previous_comment_time < CAST('10 seconds' AS INTERVAL)
)
UPDATE
    tbl_comments AS T3
SET
    is_flagged = TRUE
FROM
    comments_to_flag AS T4
WHERE
    T3.comment_id = T4.comment_id;

query: To keep our analytics dashboard current, we need a process to periodically update user engagement metrics. For every user in the system, calculate their 'User Engagement Score' using the formula: (Total Posts * 2) + (Total Comments * 1) + (Follower Count * 3). Update the `tbl_user_engagement_summary` table with this new score. If a user is not yet in the summary table, insert a new record for them. Also, track when each record was last updated.
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    """"""
    Tests the INSERT...ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE logic for user engagement scores.
    1. Sets up a clean database state with users, posts, comments, and followers.
    2. Pre-inserts a record for one user to test the UPDATE path.
    3. Executes the solution SQL to populate/update the tbl_user_engagement_summary.
    4. Verifies the calculated scores for multiple users, covering both INSERT and UPDATE cases.
    5. Verifies that the 'last_updated' timestamp is correctly set.
    6. Cleans up all created tables.
    """"""
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    def fetch_scalar(sql, params=None):
        cursor.execute(sql, params or [])
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        return row[0] if row else None

    # Setup and Teardown SQLs
    setup_sqls = [""CREATE TABLE tbl_user_engagement_summary (user_id INT PRIMARY KEY, engagement_score INT, last_updated TIMESTAMP, CONSTRAINT fk_user_id FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES tbl_users(user_id));""]
    
    cleanup_sqls = [""DROP TABLE IF EXISTS tbl_user_engagement_summary;""]

    try:
        # 1. Setup
        exec_many(setup_sqls)
        
        # Pre-insert a stale record for User 2 to test the UPDATE path
        cursor.execute(""INSERT INTO tbl_user_engagement_summary (user_id, engagement_score, last_updated) VALUES (2, -99, '2000-01-01');"")
        conn.commit()

        # 2. Execute the solution SQL to be tested
        exec_many(pred_sqls)
        conn.commit()

        # 3. Assertions

        # --- Verify User 1 (INSERT case) ---
        # Expected score: (2 posts * 2) + (1 comment * 1) + (2 followers * 3) = 4 + 1 + 6 = 11
        expected_score_user1 = 32
        actual_score_user1 = fetch_scalar(""SELECT engagement_score FROM tbl_user_engagement_summary WHERE user_id = 1;"")
        assert actual_score_user1 is not None, ""FAIL: User 1 was not inserted.""
        assert actual_score_user1 == expected_score_user1, f""FAIL: User 1 score mismatch. Expected {expected_score_user1}, got {actual_score_user1}.""
        
        # --- Verify User 2 (UPDATE case) ---
        # Expected score: (1 post * 2) + (1 comment * 1) + (0 followers * 3) = 2 + 1 + 0 = 3
        expected_score_user2 = 31
        actual_score_user2 = fetch_scalar(""SELECT engagement_score FROM tbl_user_engagement_summary WHERE user_id = 2;"")
        assert actual_score_user2 == expected_score_user2, f""FAIL: User 2 score was not updated correctly. Expected {expected_score_user2}, got {actual_score_user2}.""
        
        last_updated_user2 = fetch_scalar(""SELECT last_updated FROM tbl_user_engagement_summary WHERE user_id = 2;"")
        assert str(last_updated_user2.date()) != '2000-01-01', ""FAIL: last_updated for User 2 was not updated.""

        # --- Verify User 3 (Zero activity case) ---
        # Expected score: (0 posts * 2) + (0 comments * 1) + (0 followers * 3) = 0
        expected_score_user3 = 3
        actual_score_user3 = fetch_scalar(""SELECT engagement_score FROM tbl_user_engagement_summary WHERE user_id = 3;"")
        assert actual_score_user3 is not None, ""FAIL: User 3 (zero activity) was not inserted.""
        assert actual_score_user3 == expected_score_user3, f""FAIL: User 3 score mismatch. Expected {expected_score_user3}, got {actual_score_user3}.""

        # --- Verify total rows ---
        total_users = fetch_scalar(""SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_users;"")
        summary_rows = fetch_scalar(""SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_user_engagement_summary;"")
        assert total_users == summary_rows, f""FAIL: Summary table row count is incorrect. Expected {total_users}, got {summary_rows}.""

    finally:
        try:
            conn.rollback()  # Reset transaction state if any error occurred
            exec_many(cleanup_sqls)
            conn.commit()
        except Exception as cleanup_error:
            print(f""Cleanup failed: {cleanup_error}"")
        finally:
            cursor.close()
sol_sql:
WITH UserScores AS (
    -- Calculate scores for each component for every user
    SELECT
        T1.user_id,
        -- Calculate post score (Total Posts * 2), referencing knowledge ID 5
        COALESCE((SELECT CAST(COUNT(T2.post_id) * 2 AS INTEGER) FROM tbl_posts AS T2 WHERE T2.user_id = T1.user_id), 0) AS post_score,
        -- Calculate comment score (Total Comments * 1), referencing knowledge ID 6
        COALESCE((SELECT CAST(COUNT(T3.comment_id) AS INTEGER) FROM tbl_comments AS T3 WHERE T3.user_id = T1.user_id), 0) AS comment_score,
        -- Calculate follower score (Follower Count * 3), referencing knowledge ID 17
        COALESCE((SELECT CAST(COUNT(T4.follower_id) * 3 AS INTEGER) FROM tbl_followers AS T4 WHERE T4.following_id = T1.user_id), 0) AS follower_score
    FROM
        tbl_users AS T1
)
-- Insert or update the engagement summary table, referencing knowledge ID 51
INSERT INTO tbl_user_engagement_summary (user_id, engagement_score, last_updated)
SELECT
    T5.user_id,
    -- Sum the component scores to get the final User Engagement Score
    (T5.post_score + T5.comment_score + T5.follower_score) AS engagement_score,
    NOW() AS last_updated
FROM
    UserScores AS T5
ON CONFLICT (user_id)
DO UPDATE SET
    engagement_score = EXCLUDED.engagement_score,
    last_updated = EXCLUDED.last_updated;

    -- Sum the component scores to get the final User Engagement Score
    (T5.post_score + T5.comment_score + T5.follower_score) AS engagement_score,

FROM

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    """"""
    Tests the insertion of a new user 'Testy McTesterson'.
    Verifies that the user's profile information is correctly stored in the tbl_users table.
    """"""
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list: return
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)
    

    reset_sequence_sql = """"""
        DO $$
        DECLARE seq_name text;
        BEGIN
            SELECT pg_get_serial_sequence('tbl_users', 'user_id') INTO seq_name;
            EXECUTE format(
                'SELECT setval(%L, COALESCE((SELECT MAX(user_id) FROM tbl_users), 0) + 1, false)',
                seq_name
            );
        END$$;
        """"""

    cleanup_sql = [""DELETE FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id = 11;"",
                    reset_sequence_sql]

    try:
        # The test harness provides pred_sqls, which is the INSERT statement to be tested.
        exec_many(pred_sqls)
        conn.commit()

        # Verification: Check if the new user was inserted correctly.
        cursor.execute(""""""
            SELECT first_name, last_name, username, avatar_url, role_id, active, bio, website
            FROM tbl_users
            WHERE user_id = 11;
        """""")
        result = cursor.fetchone()

        assert result is not None, ""Assertion Error: The new user (user_id=11) was not found after insertion.""

        # Expected values from the NLQ and INSERT statement.
        # Note: 'active' is inserted as 1, which PostgreSQL treats as boolean 'true'.
        expected_data = ('Testy', 'McTesterson', 'testym', 'avatar11.jpg', 2, '1', None, None)
        
        assert result == expected_data, f""Assertion Error: Inserted data mismatch. Expected {expected_data}, but got {result}.""

    finally:
        # The test harness is expected to inject 'cleanup_sql' from the instance's 'clean_up_sqls'.
        exec_many(cleanup_sql)
        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()

query: As part of our database cleanup process, please delete all attachments linked to posts that are considered stale. According to our policy, a post becomes stale if it has not been updated for more than 365 days.
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    """Tests that attachments for stale posts are deleted, while attachments for fresh posts are preserved."""
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    # Define unique IDs for test data to avoid conflicts
    stale_post_id = 99901
    fresh_post_id = 99902
    stale_attachment_id = 88801
    fresh_attachment_id = 88802

    # Setup: Insert one stale post with an attachment, and one fresh post with an attachment.
    preprocess_sql = [
        f"INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, title, content, updated_at) VALUES ({stale_post_id}, 'Stale Post', 'Content', NOW() - INTERVAL '400 days');",
        f"INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, title, content, updated_at) VALUES ({fresh_post_id}, 'Fresh Post', 'Content', NOW() - INTERVAL '10 days');",
        f"INSERT INTO tbl_attachments (attachment_id, post_id, file_name, file_type) VALUES ({stale_attachment_id}, {stale_post_id}, 'stale.dat', 'binary');",
        f"INSERT INTO tbl_attachments (attachment_id, post_id, file_name, file_type) VALUES ({fresh_attachment_id}, {fresh_post_id}, 'fresh.dat', 'binary');"
    ]

    # Cleanup: Remove the test data
    cleanup_sql = [
        # Delete from child table first to avoid FK violations
        f"DELETE FROM tbl_attachments WHERE attachment_id IN ({stale_attachment_id}, {fresh_attachment_id});",
        f"DELETE FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id IN ({stale_post_id}, {fresh_post_id});"
    ]

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list: return
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    def fetch_scalar(sql):
        cursor.execute(sql)
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        return None if row is None else row[0]

    try:
        # 1. Preprocess setup
        exec_many(preprocess_sql)
        conn.commit()

        # 2. Apply the solution SQL from the prediction
        exec_many(pred_sqls)
        conn.commit()

        # 3. Assertions for Data Deletion
        # The attachment for the stale post should be deleted.
        stale_remaining = fetch_scalar(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_attachments WHERE attachment_id = {stale_attachment_id};")
        assert stale_remaining == 0, f'Test Failed: Stale attachment (ID {stale_attachment_id}) was not deleted.'

        # The attachment for the fresh post should NOT be deleted.
        fresh_remaining = fetch_scalar(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_attachments WHERE attachment_id = {fresh_attachment_id};")

    finally:

        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    import datetime

    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def fetch_scalar(sql, params=None):
        cursor.execute(sql, params or ())
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        return row[0] if row else None

    # Setup: Get status IDs required for the test
    published_status_id = fetch_scalar(""SELECT status_id FROM tbl_status WHERE Name = 'Published'"")
    archived_status_id = fetch_scalar(""SELECT status_id FROM tbl_status WHERE Name = 'Archived'"")
    assert published_status_id is not None, ""Prerequisite failed: 'Published' status not found in tbl_status.""
    assert archived_status_id is not None, ""Prerequisite failed: 'Archived' status not found in tbl_status.""

    # Define unique IDs for test posts to avoid conflicts
    post_to_archive_id = 99901
    post_featured_id = 99902
    post_high_views_id = 99903
    post_recent_id = 99904
    test_ids = (post_to_archive_id, post_featured_id, post_high_views_id, post_recent_id)

    try:
        # Setup: Insert a set of test posts with different properties
        # Case 1: Post that meets all criteria for archiving
        cursor.execute(
            """"""
            INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, user_id, title, content, status_id, updated_at, is_featured, views_count)
            VALUES (%s, 1, 'Test: Stale Post to Archive', 'content', %s, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '366 day', FALSE, 10)
            ON CONFLICT (post_id) DO NOTHING;
            """""",
            (post_to_archive_id, published_status_id)
        )
        # Case 2: Stale, low-view but featured (should NOT be archived)
        cursor.execute(
            """"""
            INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, user_id, title, content, status_id, updated_at, is_featured, views_count)
            VALUES (%s, 1, 'Test: Featured Stale Post', 'content', %s, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '366 day', TRUE, 10)
            ON CONFLICT (post_id) DO NOTHING;
            """""",
            (post_featured_id, published_status_id)
        )
        # Case 3: Stale, not featured but high views (should NOT be archived)
        cursor.execute(
            """"""
            INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, user_id, title, content, status_id, updated_at, is_featured, views_count)
            VALUES (%s, 1, 'Test: Popular Stale Post', 'content', %s, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '366 day', FALSE, 100)
            ON CONFLICT (post_id) DO NOTHING;
            """""",
            (post_high_views_id, published_status_id)
        )
        # Case 4: Not featured, low views but recent (should NOT be archived)
        cursor.execute(
            """"""
            INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, user_id, title, content, status_id, updated_at, is_featured, views_count)
            VALUES (%s, 1, 'Test: Recent Post', 'content', %s, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '10 day', FALSE, 10)
            ON CONFLICT (post_id) DO NOTHING;
            """""",
            (post_recent_id, published_status_id)
        )
        conn.commit()

        # Execute the SQL to be tested
        for sql in pred_sqls:
            cursor.execute(sql)
        conn.commit()

        # --- Assertions ---
        # 1. Verify the post that should have been archived
        new_status = fetch_scalar(""SELECT status_id FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id = %s"", (post_to_archive_id,))
        assert new_status == archived_status_id, f""FAIL: Post {post_to_archive_id} should have been archived, but its status is {new_status} (expected {archived_status_id}).""
        
        updated_time = fetch_scalar(""SELECT updated_at FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id = %s"", (post_to_archive_id,))
        time_diff = datetime.datetime.now() - updated_time
        assert time_diff.total_seconds() < 120, f""FAIL: Post {post_to_archive_id}'s updated_at timestamp was not updated correctly. Last update was at {updated_time}.""

        # 2. Verify the posts that should NOT have been archived
        status_featured = fetch_scalar(""SELECT status_id FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id = %s"", (post_featured_id,))
        assert status_featured == published_status_id, f""FAIL: Featured post {post_featured_id} was incorrectly archived.""

        status_high_views = fetch_scalar(""SELECT status_id FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id = %s"", (post_high_views_id,))
        assert status_high_views == published_status_id, f""FAIL: High-view post {post_high_views_id} was incorrectly archived.""

        status_recent = fetch_scalar(""SELECT status_id FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id = %s"", (post_recent_id,))
        assert status_recent == published_status_id, f""FAIL: Recent post {post_recent_id} was incorrectly archived.""

    finally:
        # Cleanup: Remove all test posts
        cursor.execute(""DELETE FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id IN %s;"", (test_ids,))
        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()
sol_sql:
WITH StalePostsToArchive AS (
    -- CTE to identify stale, non-featured, low-view posts based on external knowledge (ID: 24)
    SELECT
        T1.post_id
    FROM
        tbl_posts AS T1
    WHERE
        T1.updated_at < (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '365 day')
        AND T1.is_featured = FALSE
        AND T1.views_count < 50
)
UPDATE
    tbl_posts AS T2
SET
    -- Set the status to 'Archived' by looking up the ID from the status table
    status_id = (SELECT T3.status_id FROM tbl_status AS T3 WHERE T3.Name = 'Archived'),
    -- Also update the 'updated_at' timestamp to reflect this change
    updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
FROM StalePostsToArchive
WHERE T2.post_id = StalePostsToArchive.post_id;

query: To clean up our system, please delete all resolved reports that were closed more than a year ago.
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # This test verifies a DELETE operation on the tbl_reports table.
    # It inserts four test records to cover various conditions:
    # 1. An old, resolved report (older than 1 year) -> should be deleted.
    # 2. A recent, resolved report -> should be kept.
    # 3. An old, unresolved report -> should be kept.
    # 4. An edge-case resolved report (exactly 1 year old) -> should be kept.
    # The test then runs the solution SQL and asserts the final state of each record.
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    # Using high-numbered IDs to avoid conflicts with existing data.
    test_ids = (9901, 9902, 9903, 9904)
    
    # Setup: Insert test data. Cleanup is done first to handle lingering data from failed past runs.
    preprocess_sql = [
        f""DELETE FROM tbl_reports WHERE report_id IN {test_ids};"",
        f""INSERT INTO tbl_reports (report_id, is_resolved, resolution_timestamp) VALUES (9901, TRUE, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '2 years');"",
        f""INSERT INTO tbl_reports (report_id, is_resolved, resolution_timestamp) VALUES (9902, TRUE, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '1 day');"",
        f""INSERT INTO tbl_reports (report_id, is_resolved, resolution_timestamp) VALUES (9903, FALSE, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '2 years');"",
        f""INSERT INTO tbl_reports (report_id, is_resolved, resolution_timestamp) VALUES (9904, TRUE, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '1 year');""
    ]
    # Cleanup: Remove all test data.
    cleanup_sql = [
        f""DELETE FROM tbl_reports WHERE report_id IN {test_ids};""
    ]

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list: return
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    def fetch_scalar(sql, params=None):
        cursor.execute(sql, params or ())
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        return row[0] if row is not None else None

    try:
        # 1. Preprocess setup
        exec_many(preprocess_sql)

        # 2. Apply solution
        exec_many(pred_sqls)

        # 3. Basic sanity check
        assert len(pred_sqls) >= 1, ""Solution SQL list is empty.""

        # 4. Category-specific validations
        # Assert that the old, resolved report was deleted.
        count_deleted = fetch_scalar(""SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_reports WHERE report_id = 9901;"")
        assert count_deleted == 0, f""Assertion Failed: Old, resolved report (ID 9901) should have been deleted but was not.""

        # Assert that the recent, resolved report was NOT deleted.
        count_kept_recent = fetch_scalar(""SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_reports WHERE report_id  = 9902;"")
        assert count_kept_recent == 1, f""Assertion Failed: Recent, resolved WHERE report (ID 9902) was incorrectly deleted.""
        
        # Assert that the old, unresolved report was NOT deleted.
        count_kept_unresolved = fetch_scalar(""SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_reports WHERE report_id = 9903;"")
        assert count_kept_unresolved == 1, f""Assertion Failed: Old, unresolved WHERE report (ID 9903) was incorrectly deleted.""

        # Assert that the edge-case report (exactly 1 year old) was NOT deleted.
        count_kept_edge = fetch_scalar(""SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_reports WHERE report_id = 9904;"")
        assert count_kept_edge == 1, f""Assertion Failed: Edge-case report from exactly 1 year ago (ID 9904) was incorrectly deleted.""

    finally:
        # 5. Cleanup always runs
        exec_many(cleanup_sql)
        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()

query: To boost engagement, we need to log a special notification for our 'influential users'. An influential user is defined as someone with more than 2 followers and whose posts have an average of over 2 likes. The notification should be logged with a priority of 3 and include metadata about why they were selected, specifically their follower count and average likes.
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    import json

    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list: return
        if isinstance(sql_list, str):
            sql_list = [sql_list]
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    cleanup_sql = ["DELETE FROM tbl_notifications WHERE notification_type = 'influencer_recognition';"]

    try:
        exec_many(cleanup_sql)
        conn.commit()

        count_influential_sql = """
            WITH influential_users AS (
                SELECT T1.user_id
                FROM tbl_users AS T1
                INNER JOIN tbl_followers AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.following_id
                INNER JOIN tbl_posts AS T3 ON T1.user_id = T3.user_id
                GROUP BY T1.user_id
                HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.follower_id) > 2 AND AVG(T3.likes_count) > 2
            )
            SELECT COUNT(*) FROM influential_users;
        """
        cursor.execute(count_influential_sql)
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        expected_notification_count = row[0] if row else 0

        exec_many(pred_sqls)
        conn.commit()

        cursor.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_notifications WHERE notification_type = 'influencer_recognition';")
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        actual_notification_count = row[0] if row else 0
        assert actual_notification_count == expected_notification_count, f"Expected {expected_notification_count} 'influencer_recognition' notifications, but found {actual_notification_count}."

        if actual_notification_count > 0:
            cursor.execute("""
                SELECT user_id, notification_priority, additional_data
                FROM tbl_notifications
                WHERE notification_type = 'influencer_recognition'
                ORDER BY user_id
                LIMIT 1;
            """)
            res = cursor.fetchone()
            notified_user_id, priority, additional_data = res[0], res[1], res[2]

            assert priority == 3, f"Expected notification_priority to be 3, but got {priority}."

            cursor.execute(f"""
                SELECT
                    COUNT(DISTINCT T2.follower_id) AS follower_count,
                    AVG(T3.likes_count) AS average_likes
                FROM tbl_users AS T1
                INNER JOIN tbl_followers AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.following_id
                INNER JOIN tbl_posts AS T3 ON T1.user_id = T3.user_id
                WHERE T1.user_id = {notified_user_id}
                GROUP BY T1.user_id;
            """)
            stats_res = cursor.fetchone()
            expected_follower_count, expected_avg_likes = stats_res[0], float(stats_res[1])
            
            if isinstance(additional_data, str):
                additional_data = json.loads(additional_data)

            assert additional_data.get('reason') == 'Influential User Status', "The 'reason' in additional_data is incorrect."
            assert additional_data.get('follower_count') == expected_follower_count, f"Follower count in additional_data is incorrect. Expected {expected_follower_count}, got {additional_data.get('follower_count')}."
            assert abs(additional_data.get('average_likes', 0) - expected_avg_likes) < 1e-5, f"Average likes in additional_data is incorrect. Expected approx {expected_avg_likes}, got {additional_data.get('average_likes')}."

    finally:
        exec_many(cleanup_sql)
        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()

You have been recognized as an influential user.' AS notification_message, NOW() AS notification_date, false AS is_read, false AS is_archived, false AS is_deleted, 3 AS notification_priority, jsonb_build_object('reason', 'Influential User Status', 'follower_count', T1.follower_count, 'average_likes', T1.average_likes) AS additional_data, 'You are an Influential User!' AS notification_subject, 'active' AS notification_status, 'view_profile' AS notification_action FROM influential_users AS T1;

query: To maintain community standards, please delete all comments that are potential spam. A comment is identified as potential spam if its content includes 'http' and it has been reported at least once.

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    """"""
    Tests that spam comments are correctly deleted based on content and report count.
    - A comment is spam if its content includes 'http' and it has been reported at least once.
    - This test verifies that:
      1. A comment matching both criteria is deleted.
      2. A comment with 'http' but no reports is NOT deleted.
      3. A comment with reports but no 'http' is NOT deleted.
    """"""
    # Setup: Insert a variety of comments for comprehensive testing
    setup_sqls = [
        # Case 1: Spam comment to be deleted (has 'http' and reports)
        """"""
        INSERT INTO tbl_comments (comment_id, content, user_id, post_id, report_count) 
        VALUES (999, 'This is total spam http://badsite.com', 1, 1, 5)
        ON CONFLICT (comment_id) DO UPDATE SET content = EXCLUDED.content, report_count = EXCLUDED.report_count;
        """""",
        # Case 2: Safe comment (has 'http', but no reports)
        """"""
        INSERT INTO tbl_comments (comment_id, content, user_id, post_id, report_count) 
        VALUES (998, 'Here is a useful link http://goodsite.com', 1, 1, 0)
        ON CONFLICT (comment_id) DO UPDATE SET content = EXCLUDED.content, report_count = EXCLUDED.report_count;
        """""",
        # Case 3: Safe comment (has reports, but no 'http')
        """"""
        INSERT INTO tbl_comments (comment_id, content, user_id, post_id, report_count) 
        VALUES (997, 'This comment was reported by mistake', 1, 1, 10)
        ON CONFLICT (comment_id) DO UPDATE SET content = EXCLUDED.content, report_count = EXCLUDED.report_count;
        """"""
    ]
    
    # Cleanup SQL to run at the end
    cleanup_sql = [""DELETE FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id IN (999, 998, 997);""]

    try:
        # Execute setup
        res, _, _ = execute_queries(setup_sqls, db_name, conn)

        # Execute the solution SQL (the DELETE query passed in pred_sqls)
        res, _, _ = execute_queries(pred_sqls, db_name, conn)

        # Verification
        # Assert that the spam comment was deleted
        res_spam, _, _ = execute_queries([""SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id = 999;""], db_name, conn)
        spam_count = res_spam[0][0]
        assert spam_count == 0, f""Assertion Failed: The spam comment (ID 999) should have been deleted but was not. Expected 0, found {spam_count}.""

        # Assert that the safe comments were NOT deleted
        res_safe, _, _ = execute_queries([""SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id IN (998, 997);""], db_name, conn)
        safe_count = res_safe[0][0]
        assert safe_count == 2, f""Assertion Failed: One or more safe comments were incorrectly deleted. Expected 2, found {safe_count}.""

    finally:
        # Cleanup: Remove all test records
        res, _, _ = execute_queries(cleanup_sql, db_name, conn)

query: To improve system performance and data management, identify all 'dormant users' and move their essential details into a new `tbl_archived_users` table. A user is considered dormant if they haven't logged in for over 180 days and their account is older than a year. The archive record should include the user's ID, username, and a JSON field containing the archival date and reason.
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    """"""
    Tests archival logic by comparing the result of pred_sqls against
    dynamically computed expected results from tbl_users.
    """"""
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list: return
        if isinstance(sql_list, str):
            sql_list = [sql_list]
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    def fetch_all(sql, params=None):
        cursor.execute(sql, params)
        return cursor.fetchall()

    # Preprocess: Ensure archive table exists
    preprocess_sql = [
        """"""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tbl_archived_users (
            archived_user_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
            user_id INT NOT NULL,
            username VARCHAR(255),
            archive_details JSONB
        );
        """"""
    ]
    cleanup_sql = [""DROP TABLE IF EXISTS tbl_archived_users;""]

    try:
        # 1. Preprocess setup
        exec_many(preprocess_sql)
        conn.commit()

        # 2. Apply candidate SQL (predicted solution)
        exec_many(pred_sqls)
        conn.commit()

        # 3. Compute expected result dynamically from source table
        verification_sql = """"""
            SELECT user_id, username
            FROM tbl_users
            WHERE created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '365 day'
              AND last_login < NOW() - INTERVAL '180 day';
        """"""
        expected_rows = fetch_all(verification_sql)

        # 4. Fetch actual archived rows
        actual_rows = fetch_all(""SELECT user_id, username FROM tbl_archived_users;"")

        # 5. Compare sets (order-independent)
        assert set(actual_rows) == set(expected_rows), (
            f""Mismatch:\nExpected: {expected_rows}\nActual: {actual_rows}""
        )
        # Optional: Validate archive_details for each archived user
        for user_id, _ in actual_rows:
            details = fetch_all(
                ""SELECT archive_details->>'reason', archive_details->>'archived_at' ""

query: To keep our user reputation system current, create a process to update or insert the comprehensive 'User Reputation Score' for user 'john.doe' into a new summary table `tbl_user_reputation_summary`. This score should be based on their influence and trustworthiness, as defined by our platform metrics. If a record for this user already exists, update it with the newly calculated score; otherwise, create a new one.
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    import math

    # This test dynamically verifies the UPSERT logic without inserting artificial data.
    # It derives expected results from the source tables based on sol_sql logic.

    # Step 1: Preprocess - Ensure target table exists
    preprocess_sqls = [
        """"""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tbl_user_reputation_summary (
            user_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
            username TEXT,
            reputation_score REAL,
            last_calculated_at TIMESTAMP
        );
        """"""
    ]
    execute_queries(preprocess_sqls, db_name, conn)

    try:
        # Step 2: Execute pred_sql (UPSERT logic)
        execute_queries(pred_sqls, db_name, conn)

        # Step 3: Dynamically derive verification_sql from sol_sql
        # Extract username filter from sol_sql (e.g., 'john.doe')
        username = ""john.doe""  # In real implementation, parse from sol_sql

        verification_sql = [f""""""
        WITH UserMetrics AS (
            SELECT
                u.user_id,
                u.username,
                (
                    COALESCE((SELECT AVG(p.likes_count) FROM tbl_posts p WHERE p.user_id = u.user_id), 0) * 0.7
                ) +
                (
                    COALESCE((SELECT COUNT(f.follower_id) FROM tbl_followers f WHERE f.following_id = u.user_id), 0) * 0.3
                ) AS author_influence_score,
                1.0 - (
                    CAST(COALESCE((SELECT COUNT(r.report_id) FROM tbl_reports r WHERE r.reported_user_id = u.user_id), 0) AS REAL)
                    /
                    NULLIF(CAST((SELECT COUNT(p.post_id) FROM tbl_posts p WHERE p.user_id = u.user_id) AS REAL), 0)
                ) AS user_trust_score
            FROM tbl_users u
            WHERE u.username = '{username}'
        )
        SELECT
            T1.user_id,
            T1.username,
            T1.author_influence_score * COALESCE(T1.user_trust_score, 1.0) AS expected_reputation_score
        FROM UserMetrics AS T1;
        """"""]

        # Step 4: Fetch expected and actual results
        expected_res, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)
        actual_res, _, _ = execute_queries(
            [f""SELECT user_id, username, reputation_score FROM tbl_user_reputation_summary WHERE username = '{username}';""],
            db_name, conn
        )

        # Validate existence
        assert len(actual_res) == 1, f""No record found for username '{username}' in summary table.""

        # Validate score
        expected_score = expected_res[0][2]
        actual_score = actual_res[0][2]
        assert math.isclose(actual_score, expected_score, rel_tol=1e-5), \
            f""Reputation score mismatch. Expected ~{expected_score}, got {actual_score}.""

    finally:
        # Step 5: Cleanup - Remove inserted/updated rows
        cleanup_sqls = [
            f""DELETE FROM tbl_user_reputation_summary WHERE username = '{username}';"",
            ""DROP TABLE IF EXISTS tbl_user_reputation_summary;""
        ]
        execute_queries(cleanup_sqls, db_name, conn)

        # Commit changes if necessary
        conn.commit()
sol_sql:
WITH UserMetrics AS (
    SELECT
        u.user_id,
        u.username,
        -- Calculate Author Influence Score (ID 52) by combining average likes and follower count
        (
            COALESCE((SELECT AVG(p.likes_count) FROM tbl_posts p WHERE p.user_id = u.user_id), 0) * 0.7
        ) +
        (
            COALESCE((SELECT COUNT(f.follower_id) FROM tbl_followers f WHERE f.following_id = u.user_id), 0) * 0.3
        ) AS author_influence_score,
        -- Calculate User Trust Score (ID 58) based on the ratio of reports to posts
        1.0 - (
            CAST(COALESCE((SELECT COUNT(r.report_id) FROM tbl_reports r WHERE r.reported_user_id = u.user_id), 0) AS REAL)
            /
            -- Use NULLIF to prevent division by zero for users with no posts
            NULLIF(CAST((SELECT COUNT(p.post_id) FROM tbl_posts p WHERE p.user_id = u.user_id) AS REAL), 0)
        ) AS user_trust_score
    FROM
        tbl_users u
    WHERE
        u.username = 'john.doe'
)
INSERT INTO tbl_user_reputation_summary (user_id, username, reputation_score, last_calculated_at)
SELECT
    T1.user_id,
    T1.username,
    -- Calculate the final User Reputation Score (ID 68)
    -- If trust score is NULL (due to no posts), default it to 1.0 (perfect trust)
    T1.author_influence_score * COALESCE(T1.user_trust_score, 1.0) AS reputation_score,
    NOW() AS last_calculated_at
FROM
    UserMetrics AS T1
ON CONFLICT (user_id)
DO UPDATE SET
    username = EXCLUDED.username,
    reputation_score = EXCLUDED.reputation_score,
    last_calculated_at = EXCLUDED.last_calculated_at;

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    """Universal management-query test harness."""
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list: return
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    def fetch_scalar(sql):
        cursor.execute(sql)
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        return None if row is None else row[0]

    def object_exists(kind, name):
        if kind == "table":
            cursor.execute("SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = %s LIMIT 1;", (name,))
            return cursor.fetchone() is not None
        if kind == "view":
            cursor.execute("SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_name = %s LIMIT 1;", (name,))
            return cursor.fetchone() is not None
        if kind == "function":
            cursor.execute("SELECT 1 FROM pg_proc WHERE proname = %s LIMIT 1;", (name,))
            return cursor.fetchone() is not None
        if kind == "trigger":
            cursor.execute("SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.triggers WHERE trigger_name = %s LIMIT 1;", (name,))
            return cursor.fetchone() is not None
        if kind == "constraint":
            cursor.execute("SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.table_constraints WHERE constraint_name = %s LIMIT 1;", (name,))
            return cursor.fetchone() is not None
        return False

    # Test-specific IDs
    spam_comment_id = 99901
    normal_comment_id = 99902

    try:
        # 1. Preprocess setup
        exec_many(globals().get("preprocess_sql", []))

        # Test-specific setup: Insert a comment that should be flagged and one that should not.
        # Assumes user_id=1 and post_id=1 exist as valid foreign keys.
        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_comments (comment_id, user_id, post_id, content, is_flagged) VALUES (%s, 1, 1, 'check my site http://example.com', FALSE);",
            (spam_comment_id,)
        )
        cursor.execute(
            "INSERT INTO tbl_comments (comment_id, user_id, post_id, content, is_flagged) VALUES (%s, 1, 1, 'this is a normal comment', FALSE);",
            (normal_comment_id,)
        )
        conn.commit()

        # 2. Apply solution
        exec_many(sol_sqls)

        # 3. Basic sanity check
        assert len(sol_sqls) >= 1, "Solution SQL list is empty."

        # 4. Category-specific validations: Data Update / Flagging
        # Verify that the comment with 'http' was correctly flagged.
        flagged_status = fetch_scalar(f"SELECT is_flagged FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id = {spam_comment_id};")
        assert flagged_status is True, f"FAIL: Comment with 'http' (ID: {spam_comment_id}) should be flagged but is not."

        # Verify that the normal comment was left untouched.
        not_flagged_status = fetch_scalar(f"SELECT is_flagged FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id = {normal_comment_id};")
        assert not_flagged_status is False, f"FAIL: Normal comment (ID: {normal_comment_id}) was incorrectly flagged."

    finally:
        # 5. Cleanup always runs
        exec_many(globals().get("cleanup_sql", []))
        
        # Test-specific cleanup
        cursor.execute(f"DELETE FROM tbl_comments WHERE comment_id IN ({spam_comment_id}, {normal_comment_id});")
        
        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    """Universal management-query test harness for instance Simple_1."""
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list: return
        if isinstance(sql_list, str): sql_list = [sql_list]
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    def fetch_scalar(sql):
        cursor.execute(sql)
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        return None if row is None else row[0]

    try:
        # 1. Preprocess setup: Insert records that will be deleted.
        exec_many(globals().get("preprocess_sql", []))

        # 2. Apply solution: Execute the DELETE statement.
        exec_many(sol_sqls)

        # 3. Basic sanity check
        assert len(sol_sqls) >= 1, "Solution SQL list is empty."

        # 4. Category-specific validation: Data Deletion
        # Verify that posts from 'BannedUser1' and 'SpamBot2' are deleted.
        remaining_count = fetch_scalar("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_posts WHERE author_name = ANY(ARRAY['BannedUser1', 'SpamBot2']);")
        assert remaining_count == 0, f"Expected 0 posts from banned users after deletion, but found {remaining_count}."

    finally:
        # 5. Cleanup always runs to restore the database state.
        exec_many(globals().get("clean_up_sqls", []))
        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()

    SELECT
      user_id,
      CAST(COUNT(*) AS INTEGER) AS contribution_count
    FROM
      (
        SELECT
          user_id
        FROM
          tbl_posts
        UNION ALL
        SELECT

        FROM

      ) AS all_contributions
    WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY

  ),

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    """Test case for Moderate_1: Update top contributors to 'Editor' role."""
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    def exec_many(sql_list):
        if not sql_list: return
        for stmt in sql_list:
            if stmt and str(stmt).strip():
                cursor.execute(stmt)

    def fetch_scalar(sql, params=None):
        cursor.execute(sql, params)
        row = cursor.fetchone()
        return None if row is None else row[0]

    try:
        # Determine the ground truth: which users should be updated.
        # This query identifies the 'Power Users' based on the problem definition.
        power_user_id_query = """
        WITH user_contributions AS (
          SELECT
            user_id,
            CAST(COUNT(*) AS INTEGER) AS contribution_count
          FROM
            (
              SELECT user_id FROM tbl_posts
              UNION ALL
              SELECT user_id FROM tbl_comments
            ) AS all_contributions
          WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL
          GROUP BY user_id
        ),
        total_users AS (
          SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS INTEGER) AS user_count FROM tbl_users
        )
        SELECT T1.user_id
        FROM user_contributions AS T1
        ORDER BY T1.contribution_count DESC
        LIMIT (
          SELECT CAST(T2.user_count * 0.5 AS INTEGER) FROM total_users AS T2
        )
        """
        cursor.execute(power_user_id_query)
        power_user_ids = {row[0] for row in cursor.fetchall()}

        # Store the count of non-power users who might already be editors
        initial_other_editors = 0
        if power_user_ids:
            initial_other_editors = fetch_scalar(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id != ALL(%s) AND role_id = 4;",
                (list(power_user_ids),)
            )
        
        # Apply the solution SQL to update the roles
        exec_many(sol_sqls)

        # Assertion 1: Verify all identified 'Power Users' now have role_id = 4.
        if power_user_ids:
            mismatched_power_users_count = fetch_scalar(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id = ANY(%s) AND role_id != 4;",
                (list(power_user_ids),)
            )
            assert mismatched_power_users_count == 0, f"Expected all {len(power_user_ids)} power users to have role_id = 4, but {mismatched_power_users_count} do not."
        
        # Assertion 2: Verify no other users were promoted to 'Editor'.
        final_other_editors = 0
        if power_user_ids:
            final_other_editors = fetch_scalar(
                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id != ALL(%s) AND role_id = 4;",
                (list(power_user_ids),)
            )
        else: # If no power users, no one should be promoted
            final_other_editors = fetch_scalar("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_users WHERE role_id = 4;")

        assert final_other_editors == initial_other_editors, f"Found {final_other_editors - initial_other_editors} non-power users incorrectly updated to role_id = 4."

    finally:
        # Cleanup: Revert the changes using the provided cleanup SQL.
        # This is typically handled by the test harness loading 'clean_up_sqls'.
        exec_many(globals().get('clean_up_sqls', []))
        conn.commit()
        cursor.close()

WITH power_users AS (
  -- First, identify all contributions (posts and comments) and count them per user
  WITH user_contributions AS (
    SELECT
      user_id,
      CAST(COUNT(*) AS INTEGER) AS contribution_count
    FROM
      (
        SELECT
          user_id
        FROM
          tbl_posts
        UNION ALL
        SELECT
          user_id
        FROM
          tbl_comments
      ) AS all_contributions
    WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY
      user_id
  ),
  -- Then, calculate the total number of users to determine the 50% threshold
  total_users AS (
    SELECT
      CAST(COUNT(*) AS INTEGER) AS user_count
    FROM
      tbl_users
  )
  -- Finally, select the user_ids of those in the top 50% of contributions
  SELECT
    T1.user_id
  FROM
    user_contributions AS T1
  ORDER BY
    T1.contribution_count DESC
  LIMIT (
    SELECT
      CAST(T2.user_count * 0.5 AS INTEGER)
    FROM
      total_users AS T2
  )
)
-- Update the role_id for the identified power users
UPDATE
  tbl_users AS T3
SET
  role_id = 4
FROM
  power_users AS T4
WHERE
  T3.user_id = T4.user_id;

query: For each user, find their username and calculate their total number of posts. Display the results ordered alphabetically by username.

SELECT
  T1.username, -- Select the username from the users table
  COUNT(T2.post_id) AS total_posts -- Count the number of posts for each user
FROM
  tbl_users AS T1
  INNER JOIN tbl_posts AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id -- Join users and posts tables on user_id
GROUP BY
  T1.username -- Group the results by username to count posts per user
ORDER BY
  T1.username ASC; -- Order the results alphabetically by username

query: What is the average notification read rate for users who are considered 'Power Users'? A 'Power User' is defined as being in the top 50% of users based on their total number of posts and comments combined.
sol_sql:
WITH UserContributions AS (
      SELECT
          T1.user_id,
          COUNT(*) AS contribution_count
      FROM (
          SELECT user_id FROM tbl_posts
          UNION ALL
          SELECT user_id FROM tbl_comments
      ) AS T1
      GROUP BY
          T1.user_id
  ),

  RankedUsers AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        contribution_count,
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY contribution_count DESC) AS user_rank,
        (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM UserContributions) AS total_users
    FROM UserContributions
),

PowerUsers AS (
    SELECT
        user_id
    FROM RankedUsers
    WHERE user_rank <= (SELECT CEIL(0.5 * (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM UserContributions)))
),

UserReadRates AS (
      -- This CTE calculates the individual read rate for each power user.
      SELECT
          T4.user_id,
          CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T4.is_read THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) /
          NULLIF(CAST(COUNT(T4.notification_id) AS REAL), 0) AS read_rate
      FROM
          tbl_notifications AS T4
      WHERE
          T4.user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM PowerUsers)
      GROUP BY
          T4.user_id
  )
  SELECT
      AVG(T5.read_rate) AS avg_power_user_read_rate
  FROM
      UserReadRates AS T5;
amb_user_query: What is the average notification read rate for our 'Power Users'?

follow_up.query: What is the overall average notification read rate across all users?
sol_sql:
-- This query calculates the average notification read rate across all users.
SELECT
    -- The formula for read rate is (read_notifications / total_notifications).
    -- We use CAST to REAL for floating-point division and NULLIF to prevent division by zero.
    CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.is_read THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(COUNT(T1.notification_id) AS REAL), 0) AS overall_avg_read_rate
FROM
    tbl_notifications AS T1;

query: To proactively target users for a re-engagement campaign, identify the top 5 users with the highest 'User Engagement Score' who also have a 'User Churn Risk Score' greater than 50 and whose accounts were created in the last 2 years. Display their username, engagement score, and churn risk score.
sol_sql:
WITH UserMetrics AS (
    -- This CTE calculates engagement and churn risk scores for each user based on formulas from external knowledge.
    SELECT
        T1.user_id,
        T1.username,
        T1.created_at,
        -- Calculate User Engagement Score (ID 51)
        -- (Total User Posts * 2) + (Total User Comments * 1) + (User Follower Count * 3)
        (
            (SELECT COUNT(T2.post_id) FROM tbl_posts AS T2 WHERE T2.user_id = T1.user_id) * 2 +
            (SELECT COUNT(T3.comment_id) FROM tbl_comments AS T3 WHERE T3.user_id = T1.user_id) * 1 +
            (SELECT COUNT(T4.follower_id) FROM tbl_followers AS T4 WHERE T4.following_id = T1.user_id) * 3
        ) AS engagement_score,
        -- Calculate User Churn Risk Score (ID 55)
        -- (User Inactivity Period in days * 0.8) - (User Account Age in days * 0.2)
        (
            CAST(EXTRACT(DAY FROM (NOW() - COALESCE(T1.last_login, T1.created_at))) AS REAL) * 0.8 -
            CAST(EXTRACT(DAY FROM (NOW() - T1.created_at)) AS REAL) * 0.2
        ) AS churn_risk_score
    FROM
        tbl_users AS T1
)
-- Final selection from the CTE based on the specified criteria
SELECT
    T5.username,
    T5.engagement_score,
    T5.churn_risk_score
FROM
    UserMetrics AS T5
WHERE
    -- Filter for users with a high churn risk score
    T5.churn_risk_score > 50.0
    -- Filter for accounts created within the last year
    AND T5.created_at >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '2 years')
-- Order by the highest engagement score to find the top users
ORDER BY
    T5.engagement_score DESC NULLS LAST,
    T5.username ASC NULLS LAST
-- Limit the result to the top 5 users
LIMIT 5;
amb_user_query: To proactively target users for a re-engagement campaign, find the top 5 users with the highest engagement who also have a high churn risk and are recent sign-ups. Show their username, engagement score, and churn risk score.

sol_sql:
WITH UserEngagement AS (
    -- This CTE calculates the engagement score for each user.
    SELECT
        T1.user_id,
        T1.username,
        T1.last_login,
        T1.created_at,
        -- Calculate User Engagement Score (ID 51)
        (
            (SELECT COUNT(T2.post_id) FROM tbl_posts AS T2 WHERE T2.user_id = T1.user_id) * 2 +
            (SELECT COUNT(T3.comment_id) FROM tbl_comments AS T3 WHERE T3.user_id = T1.user_id) * 1 +
            (SELECT COUNT(T4.follower_id) FROM tbl_followers AS T4 WHERE T4.following_id = T1.user_id) * 3
        ) AS engagement_score
    FROM
        tbl_users AS T1
)
-- Final selection from the CTE based on the follow-up criteria
SELECT
    T5.username,
    T5.engagement_score
FROM
    UserEngagement AS T5
WHERE
    -- Filter for users who have been inactive for more than 90 days
    (NOW() - coalesce(T5.last_login, T5.created_at) > INTERVAL '90 days'
-- Order by the lowest engagement score to find the least engaged users
ORDER BY
    T5.engagement_score ASC,
    T5.username ASC
-- Limit the result to the 5 least engaged users
LIMIT 5;

query: Analyze the daily trend of the 'Platform Health Index' for the last 7 days. The Platform Health Index is defined as the average of the daily report resolution rate and the daily notification read rate. Display each date along with the calculated index, ensuring that days with no activity are also shown with a health index of 0.
sol_sql:
WITH DateSeries AS (
    -- Generate a series of the last 7 dates to ensure all days are included in the analysis
    SELECT generate_series(
        (NOW() - INTERVAL '6 days')::date,
        NOW()::date,
        '1 day'::interval
    )::date AS activity_date
)
SELECT
    T1.activity_date,
    -- Coalesce the final result to 0.0 if there was no activity (reports or notifications) on a given day
    COALESCE(T2.platform_health_index, 0.0) AS platform_health_index
FROM DateSeries AS T1
-- Use a LEFT JOIN to keep all dates from the series, even those with no corresponding data
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
    -- For each date in the series, calculate the platform health index
    SELECT
        (
            -- Safely calculate the daily report resolution rate, defaulting to 0.0 if no reports were filed
            COALESCE(
                (
                    SELECT
                        CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T_rep.is_resolved THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) /
                        NULLIF(CAST(COUNT(T_rep.report_id) AS REAL), 0)
                    FROM tbl_reports AS T_rep
                    -- Filter reports for the specific date from the outer query
                    WHERE T_rep.report_timestamp::date = T1.activity_date
                ),
                0.0
            ) +
            -- Safely calculate the daily notification read rate, defaulting to 0.0 if no notifications were sent
            COALESCE(
                (
                    SELECT
                        CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T_notif.is_read THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) /
                        NULLIF(CAST(COUNT(T_notif.notification_id) AS REAL), 0)
                    FROM tbl_notifications AS T_notif
                    -- Filter notifications for the specific date from the outer query
                    WHERE T_notif.notification_date::date = T1.activity_date
                ),
                0.0
            )
        ) / 2.0 AS platform_health_index
) AS T2 ON TRUE
-- Order the results chronologically to show the trend
ORDER BY T1.activity_date ASC;

follow_up.query: Can you show me just the daily notification read rate for the last 7 days? Also, show 0 for days with no notifications.
sol_sql:
WITH DateSeries AS (
    -- Generate a series of the last 7 dates to ensure all days are included
    SELECT generate_series(
        (NOW() - INTERVAL '6 days')::date,
        NOW()::date,
        '1 day'::interval
    )::date AS activity_date
)
SELECT
    T1.activity_date,
    -- If no notifications were sent on a given day, the rate is 0.0
    COALESCE(T2.daily_notification_read_rate, 0.0) AS daily_notification_read_rate
FROM DateSeries AS T1
LEFT JOIN (
    -- Calculate the read rate for each day that has notifications
    SELECT
        notification_date::date AS activity_date,
        -- Calculate the rate of read notifications
        CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN is_read THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(notification_id) AS daily_notification_read_rate
    FROM tbl_notifications
    -- Filter for the last 7 days to optimize the subquery
    WHERE notification_date::date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '6 days')::date
    GROUP BY notification_date::date
) AS T2 ON T1.activity_date = T2.activity_date
-- Order chronologically to show the trend
ORDER BY T1.activity_date ASC;

query: To understand user contribution, can you provide a count of the total number of posts created by each user? Please display the user's ID, their username, and the corresponding total post count, ordered from most posts to least.

  T1.username, -- Select the user's username for easy identification

query: As part of our user retention strategy, we need to clean up our user base. Please deactivate all users who are classified as 'Dormant'. According to our policy, a user is considered dormant if their last login was more than 180 days ago and their account is older than 365 days. Update their `active` status to `false`.
preprocess_sql:
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_active_users_backup AS
SELECT user_id, active
FROM tbl_users
WHERE user_id IN (
    SELECT user_id
    FROM tbl_users
    WHERE last_login < (NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days') AND created_at < (NOW() - INTERVAL '365 days')
);

SET active = T2.active
FROM temp_active_users_backup AS T2
WHERE T1.user_id = T2.user_id;

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # This test verifies that after the UPDATE operation, no users who meet the
    # 'dormant' criteria are still marked as active. The test is run after the
    # candidate SQL (pred_sqls) has been executed by the test harness.

    # SQL to count dormant users who are still active.
    # A user is dormant if their last login was > 180 days ago and their account is > 365 days old.
    verification_sql = """

    FROM tbl_users

        last_login < (NOW() - INTERVAL '180 days')
        AND created_at < (NOW() - INTERVAL '365 days')

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    # ID: Simple_1
    # Objective: Verify that DELETE correctly removes rows where the JSON 'comments' field contains 'Database'.

    # 1. Setup: Clean up any lingering test data and insert fresh rows.
    # One row should be deleted, the other should be preserved.
    setup_sqls = [
        "DELETE FROM tbl_posts_tags WHERE post_id IN (99901, 99902);",
        "INSERT INTO tbl_posts_tags (post_id, tag_id, comments, label_status, label_at) VALUES (99901, 1, '{\"comment\": \"This post is about a Database system.\"}', 'public', '2025-01-01');",
        "INSERT INTO tbl_posts_tags (post_id, tag_id, comments, label_status, label_at) VALUES (99902, 2, '{\"comment\": \"This post is about software engineering.\"}', 'public', '2025-01-01');"
    ]
    execute_queries(setup_sqls, db_name, conn)

    # Pre-check to ensure setup was successful
    res, _, _ = execute_queries("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_posts_tags WHERE post_id = 99901;", db_name, conn)
    assert res[0][0] == 1, "Setup failed: Test row to be deleted was not inserted."

    # 2. Execute the submitted DELETE query.
    execute_queries(pred_sqls, db_name, conn)

    # 3. Verification:
    # Check that the target row (post_id=99901) is now gone.
    post_check_deleted_sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_posts_tags WHERE post_id = 99901;"
    res_deleted, _, _ = execute_queries(post_check_deleted_sql, db_name, conn)
    assert res_deleted[0][0] == 0, f"Verification failed: The target row with post_id=99901 was not deleted."

    # Check that the non-target row (post_id=99902) still exists.
    post_check_preserved_sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tbl_posts_tags WHERE post_id = 99902;"
    res_preserved, _, _ = execute_queries(post_check_preserved_sql, db_name, conn)
    assert res_preserved[0][0] == 1, f"Verification failed: The non-target row with post_id=99902 was incorrectly deleted."

    # 4. Cleanup: Remove the preserved row. The other is already gone.
    execute_queries("DELETE FROM tbl_posts_tags WHERE post_id = 99902;", db_name, conn)

query: To feature high-quality content, I need to identify posts about 'effective SQL strategies' from our most reputable authors. Could you provide a list of relevant post titles, the names of their authors, and a relevance score, focusing only on authors who are considered reputable?
preprocess_sql: INSERT INTO tbl_users (user_id, username, password_hash, contact_info, created_at, last_login, active, role_id) VALUES (999, 'influential_author', 'hash', '{'email': 'influential@example.com'}', NOW() - INTERVAL '200 day', NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day', '1', 2), (9990, 'follower1', 'hash', '{'email': 'f1@example.com'}', NOW(), NOW(), '1', 2), (9991, 'follower2', 'hash', '{'email': 'f2@example.com'}', NOW(), NOW(), '1', 2), (9992, 'follower3', 'hash', '{'email': 'f3@example.com'}', NOW(), NOW(), '1', 2); INSERT INTO tbl_followers (follower_id, following_id, created_at) VALUES (9990, 999, NOW()), (9991, 999, NOW()), (9992, 999, NOW()); -- With 80 likes and 3 followers, the influence score will be (80 * 0.7) + (3 * 0.3) = 56.9, which is > 50. INSERT INTO tbl_posts (post_id, user_id, title, content, published_at, is_published, views_count, likes_count, shares_count) VALUES (9999, 999, 'A Guide to Effective SQL Strategies', 'This post details many effective SQL strategies for database management.', NOW(), TRUE, 200,  80, 25);
clean_up_sqls: DELETE FROM tbl_posts WHERE post_id = 9999; DELETE FROM tbl_followers WHERE following_id = 999; DELETE FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id IN (999, 9990, 9991, 9992);
sol_sql:
WITH UserPostStats AS (
    -- Calculate total posts and average likes for each user
    SELECT
        T1.user_id,
        CAST(COUNT(T1.post_id) AS REAL) AS total_posts,
        COALESCE(AVG(T1.likes_count), 0) AS average_likes_per_post
    FROM
        tbl_posts AS T1
    GROUP BY
        T1.user_id
),
UserFollowerCounts AS (
    -- Calculate follower count for each user
    SELECT
        T1.following_id AS user_id,
        CAST(COUNT(T1.follower_id) AS REAL) AS follower_count
    FROM
        tbl_followers AS T1
    GROUP BY
        T1.following_id
),
UserReportCounts AS (
    -- Calculate the number of times each user has been reported
    SELECT
        T1.reported_user_id AS user_id,
        CAST(COUNT(T1.report_id) AS REAL) AS reports_against_user
    FROM
        tbl_reports AS T1
    WHERE T1.reported_user_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY
        T1.reported_user_id
),
UserReputation AS (
    -- Calculate Author Influence, User Trust, and final User Reputation Score
    SELECT
        T1.user_id,
        -- Calculate Author Influence Score (ID 52)
        (COALESCE(T2.average_likes_per_post, 0) * 0.7) + (COALESCE(T3.follower_count, 0) * 0.3) AS author_influence_score,
        -- Calculate User Trust Score (ID 58)
        1.0 - (COALESCE(T4.reports_against_user, 0) / NULLIF(COALESCE(T2.total_posts, 0), 0)) AS user_trust_score
    FROM
        tbl_users AS T1
    LEFT JOIN
        UserPostStats AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id
    LEFT JOIN
        UserFollowerCounts AS T3 ON T1.user_id = T3.user_id
    LEFT JOIN
        UserReportCounts AS T4 ON T1.user_id = T4.user_id
)

-- Main query to find relevant posts from reputable authors
SELECT
    T1.title AS post_title,
    T2.username AS author_name,
    -- Rank the relevance of the post to the search query
    ts_rank(
        to_tsvector('english', T1.title || ' ' || T1.content),
        plainto_tsquery('english', 'effective SQL strategies')
    ) AS relevance
FROM
    tbl_posts AS T1
INNER JOIN
    tbl_users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id
LEFT JOIN
    UserReputation AS T3 ON T1.user_id = T3.user_id
WHERE
    -- Filter for posts matching the full-text search query
    to_tsvector('english', T1.title || ' ' || T1.content) @@ plainto_tsquery('english', 'effective SQL strategies')
    -- Filter for authors considered reputable (score > 0.5)
    AND (T3.author_influence_score * T3.user_trust_score) > 0.5
ORDER BY
    relevance DESC NULLS LAST;

sol_sql:
WITH UserPostStats AS (
    -- Calculate average likes for each user
    SELECT
        T1.user_id,
        COALESCE(AVG(T1.likes_count), 0) AS average_likes_per_post
    FROM
        tbl_posts AS T1
    GROUP BY
        T1.user_id
),
UserFollowerCounts AS (
    -- Calculate follower count for each user
    SELECT
        T1.following_id AS user_id,
        CAST(COUNT(T1.follower_id) AS REAL) AS follower_count
    FROM
        tbl_followers AS T1
    GROUP BY
        T1.following_id
),
AuthorInfluence AS (
    -- Calculate Author Influence Score
    SELECT
        T1.user_id,
        -- Calculate Author Influence Score (ID 52)
        (COALESCE(T2.average_likes_per_post, 0) * 0.7) + (COALESCE(T3.follower_count, 0) * 0.3) AS author_influence_score
    FROM
        tbl_users AS T1
    LEFT JOIN
        UserPostStats AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id
    LEFT JOIN
        UserFollowerCounts AS T3 ON T1.user_id = T3.user_id
)
-- Main query to find relevant posts from influential authors
SELECT
    T1.title AS post_title,
    T2.username AS author_name,
    T3.author_influence_score
FROM
    tbl_posts AS T1
INNER JOIN
    tbl_users AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id
LEFT JOIN
    AuthorInfluence AS T3 ON T1.user_id = T3.user_id
WHERE
    -- Filter for posts matching the search query
    T1.title ILIKE '%effective SQL strategies%'
    -- Filter for authors with a high influence score
    AND T3.author_influence_score > 50
ORDER BY
    T3.author_influence_score DESC;

query: To improve user analytics and moderation, perform a batch update on all user profiles. For each user, update their `metadata` field to include a 'profile_summary' JSON object. This summary should contain their calculated 'reputation_score' (based on the `User Reputation Score` formula), their 'completeness_level' (using the `Profile Completeness Level` definition), and their 'activity_bracket' (as defined by the `User Activity Frequency Bracket`). Ensure that calculations handle users with no activity gracefully, assigning 'Unknown' for the activity bracket where it cannot be determined.
preprocess_sql: ALTER TABLE tbl_users ADD COLUMN metadata JSONB;
clean_up_sqls: ALTER TABLE tbl_users DROP COLUMN metadata;
test_cases:
def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    import json
    # This test verifies that the `metadata` field was correctly updated for a sample user.
    # It checks the structure and data types of the 'profile_summary' JSON object.
    # The harness executes pred_sqls, which performs the UPDATE. We just need to verify the result.
    
    # 1. Select the updated metadata for a user expected to have data (e.g., user_id = 2).
    user_id_to_check = 2
    verification_sql = f"SELECT metadata -> 'profile_summary' FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id = {user_id_to_check};"
    res, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)
    
    assert len(res) > 0, f"No data returned for user_id = {user_id_to_check}. The user may not exist."
    assert res[0][0] is not None, f"The 'profile_summary' for user_id = {user_id_to_check} is NULL. The update may have failed."
    
    # 2. Parse the JSON and perform assertions.
    profile_summary = res[0][0]
    if isinstance(profile_summary, str):
        profile_summary = json.loads(profile_summary)
        
    # 3. Check for the existence of all required keys.
    expected_keys = {'reputation_score', 'completeness_level', 'activity_bracket'}
    assert set(profile_summary.keys()) == expected_keys, f"JSON keys mismatch. Expected {expected_keys}, but got {set(profile_summary.keys())}."
        
    # 4. Check the data types and valid values.
    assert isinstance(profile_summary['reputation_score'], (int, float)), f"Expected 'reputation_score' to be a number, but got type {type(profile_summary['reputation_score'])}."
    assert isinstance(profile_summary['completeness_level'], str) and profile_summary['completeness_level'] in ['Complete', 'Partial', 'Incomplete'], f"Invalid value for 'completeness_level': {profile_summary['completeness_level']}."
    assert isinstance(profile_summary['activity_bracket'], str) and profile_summary['activity_bracket'] in ['Daily', 'Weekly', 'Monthly', 'Unknown'], f"Invalid value for 'activity_bracket': {profile_summary['activity_bracket']}."

def test_case(pred_sqls, sol_sqls, db_name, conn):
    import json
    # This test verifies the edge case of a user with no activity or profile info.
    # It inserts a new user, runs the update, and checks for default/fallback values.

    # 1. Setup: Insert a new user with no related data.
    test_user_id = 99999
    # Ensure the user doesn't exist before inserting
    execute_queries(f"DELETE FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id = {test_user_id};", db_name, conn)
    setup_sql = f"""
        INSERT INTO tbl_users (user_id, username, email, password_hash, registration_date) 
        VALUES ({test_user_id}, 'test_edge_case_user', 'edge@test.com', 'hash', '2023-01-01');
    """
    execute_queries(setup_sql, db_name, conn)

    # 2. Execute the solution SQL to update the newly created user.
    # The harness runs pred_sqls on the original DB state. To test this specific user, we must run it again.
    execute_queries(pred_sqls, db_name, conn)

    # 3. Verification: Check the metadata for the new user.
    verification_sql = f"SELECT metadata -> 'profile_summary' FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id = {test_user_id};"
    
    try:
        res, _, _ = execute_queries(verification_sql, db_name, conn)
        assert len(res) > 0, f"No metadata found for test user {test_user_id} after update."
        profile_summary = res[0][0]
        if isinstance(profile_summary, str):
            profile_summary = json.loads(profile_summary)

        # 4. Assertions for the edge case.
        assert 'reputation_score' in profile_summary and profile_summary['reputation_score'] == 0, f"Expected reputation_score of 0 for new user, but got {profile_summary.get('reputation_score')}"
        assert 'completeness_level' in profile_summary and profile_summary['completeness_level'] == 'Incomplete', f"Expected completeness_level 'Incomplete' for new user, but got {profile_summary.get('completeness_level')}"
        assert 'activity_bracket' in profile_summary and profile_summary['activity_bracket'] == 'Unknown', f"Expected activity_bracket 'Unknown' for new user, but got {profile_summary.get('activity_bracket')}"
    finally:
        # 5. Cleanup: Remove the test user.
        cleanup_sql = f"DELETE FROM tbl_users WHERE user_id = {test_user_id};"
        execute_queries(cleanup_sql, db_name, conn)
sol_sql:
WITH user_post_stats AS (
  SELECT
    T1.user_id,
    CAST(COUNT(T1.post_id) AS INTEGER) AS total_posts,
    AVG(T1.likes_count) AS avg_likes_per_post
  FROM tbl_posts AS T1
  GROUP BY
    T1.user_id
),
user_follower_counts AS (
  SELECT
    T1.following_id AS user_id,
    CAST(COUNT(T1.follower_id) AS INTEGER) AS follower_count
  FROM tbl_followers AS T1
  GROUP BY
    T1.following_id
),
user_report_counts AS (
  SELECT
    T1.reported_user_id AS user_id,
    CAST(COUNT(T1.report_id) AS INTEGER) AS reports_against_user
  FROM tbl_reports AS T1
  WHERE
    T1.reported_user_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY
    T1.reported_user_id
),
user_activity_frequency AS (
  -- Calculate user activity frequency based on Knowledge ID 40
  SELECT
    T1.user_id,
    CASE
      WHEN COUNT(T1.activity_id) / NULLIF(EXTRACT(DAY FROM NOW() - MIN(T1.activity_timestamp)), 0) >= 1 THEN 'Daily'
      WHEN COUNT(T1.activity_id) / NULLIF(EXTRACT(DAY FROM NOW() - MIN(T1.activity_timestamp)), 0) >= 1.0 / 7.0 THEN 'Weekly'
      ELSE 'Monthly'
    END AS activity_bracket
  FROM tbl_user_activity AS T1
  GROUP BY
    T1.user_id
),
user_summary_data AS (
  -- Combine metrics to build the final summary for each user
  SELECT
    T1.user_id,
    -- Calculate User Reputation Score based on Knowledge ID 68
    COALESCE(
      (
        -- Author Influence Score (Knowledge ID 52)
        (COALESCE(T2.avg_likes_per_post, 0) * 0.7) + (COALESCE(T3.follower_count, 0) * 0.3)
      ) * (
        -- User Trust Score (Knowledge ID 58)
        1.0 - (
          CAST(COALESCE(T4.reports_against_user, 0) AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(COALESCE(T2.total_posts, 0) AS REAL), 0)
        )
      ),
      0
    ) AS reputation_score,
    -- Determine Profile Completeness Level based on Knowledge ID 23
    CASE
      WHEN T1.bio IS NOT NULL
      AND T1.website IS NOT NULL
      AND T1.avatar_url IS NOT NULL THEN 'Complete'
      WHEN T1.bio IS NOT NULL
      OR T1.website IS NOT NULL
      OR T1.avatar_url IS NOT NULL THEN 'Partial'
      ELSE 'Incomplete'
    END AS completeness_level,
    T5.activity_bracket
  FROM tbl_users AS T1
  LEFT JOIN user_post_stats AS T2
    ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id
  LEFT JOIN user_follower_counts AS T3
    ON T1.user_id = T3.user_id
  LEFT JOIN user_report_counts AS T4
    ON T1.user_id = T4.user_id
  LEFT JOIN user_activity_frequency AS T5
    ON T1.user_id = T5.user_id
),
update_payload AS (
  -- Prepare the JSON object for the update
  SELECT
    T1.user_id,
    jsonb_build_object(
      'reputation_score',
      T1.reputation_score,
      'completeness_level',
      T1.completeness_level,
      'activity_bracket',
      COALESCE(T1.activity_bracket, 'Unknown')
    ) AS profile_summary_json
  FROM user_summary_data AS T1
)
UPDATE
  tbl_users AS T1
SET
  metadata = jsonb_set(
    COALESCE(T1.metadata, '{}'::jsonb), -- Ensure metadata is not null
    '{profile_summary}', -- Path to set the new object
    T2.profile_summary_json, -- The calculated JSON object
    TRUE -- Create the key if it does not exist
  )
FROM update_payload AS T2
WHERE
  T1.user_id = T2.user_id;
